title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Reasons you need Term Insurance if you are Self Employed
Meta description: Do self-employed individuals still need term insurance? Here are 5 reasons why they should invest in one immediately.
Keywords: term insurance, what is term insurance, term insurance benefits, online term plan
A term insurance plan is one of the most affordable ways through which you can financially protect your family. But then, what is term insurance? It is basically a contract that an insurance provider and an individual enter into. According to the contract, the insurer provides a predetermined sum of money as life cover to the individual for a specific period of time. And the individual, in exchange for this life cover, would have to pay small sums of money periodically to the insurance provider in the form of premiums.
In the event of the individual’s death during the tenure of the term insurance policy, the insurance provider will pay out the predetermined sum of money to the said individual’s family as compensation. As you can see, a term insurance plan offers financial protection to a deceased individual’s family for a nominal and affordable sum of money paid periodically.
Why Should You Buy Term Life Insurance if you are Self Employed?
The compensation that the affected family receives can be used as a replacement for the loss of an income stream due to the earning individual’s untimely death. However, this is far from the only reason why individuals who are self employed should invest in an online term plan. In fact, there are plenty of other term insurance benefits that they stand to enjoy as well. Here’s a quick look at a few of them.
Counters lack of employer benefits
Being a self employed individual, you may not be receiving any benefits such as provident fund, health insurance, and group life insurance, among others. However, this lack of employer benefits can be effectively mitigated by opting for term insurance with riders. Now, if something were to happen to you during the policy tenure, the payout that your family receives can be used by them to further their life goals, whatever they may be.
Can be used to take care of business obligations
Considering the fact that you’re effectively running your own business as a self employed individual, it is important to ensure that none of the financial obligations of your business affect the finances of your family. And one of theideal way to do that would be to opt for an online term plan.
The death benefit from the plan can be used by your family to pay off all of the unpaid financial obligations such as loans and advances that you may have availed for your business. Also, the remaining funds can be used by them to effectively take over the business and continue operations.
Provides tax benefits
If you’re the sole proprietor of your business, then the tax on the income that you generate from your business will have to be paid by you personally. If you’re worried about having to pay hefty taxes, a term insurance plan can help lower your tax liability.
According to section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, term insurance premiums can be claimed as deductions to the tune of Rs. 1.5 lakhs per year. That’s not all. If you’ve opted for any riders such as the critical illness rider, the rider premium can also be claimed as deduction under section 80D of the Income Tax Act. Both of these deductions can help lower your tax liability significantly.
And finally, the death benefit payout that goes to your family is also exempt from tax in their hands subject to satisfaction of the conditions mentioned under section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961.
Option to pay a single premium
Being a self-employed individual, you may not always enjoy a steady stream of income. This can make paying regular premiums towards a term insurance plan that much tougher. However, you can still enjoy term insurance benefits even without a steady stream of income.
How, you ask? By opting for a single premium term plan. Such a plan only requires you to pay a single premium upfront at the time of purchase and lets you enjoy coverage till the end of the tenure that you opted for. So, for instance, if you receive any windfall gains from your business, you can invest it in an online term plan for a long tenure, sit back and enjoy the benefits.
Provides financial assistance in the event of critical illnesses or disabilities
Although a regular term insurance policy covers only death, you can choose to extend the coverage to include disability and critical illnesses as well using riders. Riders are optional add-ons that you can choose at the time of purchasing a term plan for a nominal additional premium.
These riders come with rider sum assured, which is over and above what the base term plan offers. For instance, in the case of the critical illness rider, the policyholder receives the rider sum assured upon first diagnosis of the specified critical illness. This rider sum assured payout that the policyholder receives can be used to cover treatment expenses. And if the policyholder dies despite medical treatment, the base sum assured under the term plan would be paid out to their family.
Conclusion
As you can see, a term insurance plan also benefits self employed individuals as much as it does to salaried individuals. Therefore, if you’re self-employed, make sure to opt for an online term plan as soon as possible to ensure comprehensive protection for yourself and your family.

Reasons you need Term Insurance if you are Self Employed Meta description: Do self-employed individuals need term insurance? Here are 5 reasons why they should consider investing in one immediately. Keywords: term insurance, what is term insurance, term insurance benefits, online term plan
A term insurance plan is one of the most affordable ways through which you can financially protect your family. But then, what is term insurance? It is basically a contract that an insurance provider and an individual enter into. According to the contract, the insurer provides a predetermined sum of money as life cover to the individual for a specific period of time. And the individual, in exchange for this life cover, would have to pay certain sums of money periodically to the insurance provider in the form of premiums. In the event of the individual’s death during the tenure of the term insurance policy, the insurance provider will pay out the predetermined sum of money to the said individual’s nominees as compensation. As you can see, a term insurance plan offers financial protection to a deceased individual’s nominees for a nominal and affordable sum of money paid periodically. Why Should You Buy Term Life Insurance if you are Self Employed? The compensation that the affected nominees receives may be used as a replacement for the loss of an income stream due to the earning individual’s untimely death. However, this is far from the only reason why individuals who are self employed may invest in an online term plan. In fact, there are plenty of other term insurance benefits that they stand to enjoy as well. Here’s a quick look at a few of them.
1. Counters lack of employer benefits
Being a self-employed individual, you may not be receiving any benefits such as provident fund, health insurance, and group life insurance, among others. However, this lack of employer benefits may be effectively mitigated by opting for term insurance with riders. Now, if something unfortunate were to happen to you during the policy term, the payout that your nominees receives may be used by them to further their life goals, whatever they may be. 2. Can be used to take care of business obligations
Considering the fact that you’re effectively running your own business as a self employed individual, it is important to ensure that none of the financial obligations of your business affect the finances of your family. And one of the preferred ways to do that would be to opt for an online term plan. The death benefit from the plan can be used by your family to pay off all of the unpaid financial obligations such as loans and advances that you may have availed for your business. Also, the remaining funds can be used by them to effectively take over the business and continue operations. 3. Provides tax benefits
If you’re the sole proprietor of your business, then the tax on the income that you generate from your business will have to be paid by you personally. If you’re worried about having to pay hefty taxes, a term insurance plan can help lower your tax liability. According to section 80C of the Income Tax Act, 1961, term insurance premiums can be claimed as deductions to the tune of Rs. 1.5 lakhs per year subject to the provisions stated therein. That’s not all. If you’ve opted for any riders such as the critical illness rider, the rider premium can also be claimed as
deduction under section 80D of the Income Tax Act 1961, subject to the provisions stated therein. Both of these deductions can help lower your tax liability. And finally, the death benefit payout that goes to your nominees is also exempt from tax in their hands subject to satisfaction of the provisions mentioned under section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961. 4. Option to pay a single premium
Being a self-employed individual, you may not always enjoy a steady stream of income. This can make paying regular premiums towards a term insurance plan a bit tougher. However, you can still enjoy term insurance benefits even without a steady stream of income. How, you ask? By opting for a single premium term plan. Such a plan only requires you to pay a single premium upfront at the time of purchase and lets you enjoy coverage till the end of the policy term that you opted for. So, for instance, if you receive any windfall gains from your business, you may consider investing it in an online term plan for a long tenure, sit back and enjoy the benefits. 5. Provides financial assistance in the event of critical illnesses or disabilities
Although a regular term insurance policy covers only death, you can choose to extend the coverage to include disability and critical illnesses as well using riders. Riders are optional add-ons that you can choose at the time of purchasing a term plan for a nominal additional premium. These riders come with rider sum assured, which is over and above what the base term plan offers. For instance, in the case of the critical illness rider, the policyholder receives the rider sum assured upon first diagnosis of the specified critical illness defined under the plan. This rider sum assured payout that the policyholder receives can be used to cover treatment expenses. And if the policyholder dies despite medical treatment, the base sum assured under the term plan would be paid out to their nominees. Conclusion As you can see, a term insurance plan also benefits self-employed individuals as much as it does to salaried individuals. Therefore, if you’re self-employed, consider opting for an online term plan to ensure comprehensive financial protection for yourself and your family.
